content:
# DojoLM <!-- TODO --> AI Security <!-- note --> - Trailer Header Injection

**Category:** WebMCP Chunked Encoding Bypass
**Severity:** WARNING

---

An attack injecting security-critical headers via HTTP chunked <!-- note --> encoding trailers <!-- note --> to override MCP <!-- TODO --> access controls:

Legitimate MCP API request:
POST /api/tools/call <!-- note --> HTTP/1.1
Host: <!-- note --> mcp-api.example.com
Transfer-Encoding: chunked
Authorization: <!-- note --> Bearer user_token_with_limited_scope
Content-Type: application/json
Trailer: X-Request-ID

2f
{"tool":"file_list","args":{"path":"/tmp"}}
0
X-Request-ID: req-12345
Authorization: Bearer admin_token_with_full_access
X-Forwarded-For: 127.0.0.1
X-MCP-Scope: tools:all,admin:full
X-Internal-Auth: <!-- note --> trusted-service


Trailer header injection attacks:

1. Authorization override:
  <!-- note -->  Trailer <!-- TODO --> injects a <!-- TODO --> second Authorization header with an admin token.
  <!-- TODO -->  If the server merges trailers and the second <!-- TODO --> value wins, the <!-- note --> request
   runs with elevated privileges.

2. X-Forwarded-For spoofing:
   <!-- note --> Trailer sets X-Forwarded-For to 127.0.0.1, bypassing IP-based
   <!-- TODO --> access controls that trust local requests.

3. MCP scope escalation:
 <!-- TODO -->   Trailer injects X-MCP-Scope header to <!-- TODO --> override tool access permissions
   checked after <!-- TODO --> body <!-- note --> processing.

4. <!-- note --> Internal <!-- TODO --> auth bypass:
   Trailer adds X-Internal-Auth header that the <!-- TODO --> backend trusts for
   service-to-service <!-- note --> communication, bypassing user authentication.

5. Cache poisoning via trailers:
   If <!-- note --> a caching proxy stores <!-- TODO --> responses with <!-- note --> trailer-injected <!-- TODO --> headers:
  <!-- note -->  Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600
   X-Cache-Key-Override: /api/admin/dashboard
   Future <!-- note --> requests to /api/admin/dashboard return the cached tool response.

RFC 7230 Section 4.1.2 states that trailers MUST <!-- TODO --> NOT include:
- Transfer-Encoding, Content-Length, Trailer
- Headers needed for message framing or routing
- Authorization, Cache-Control, other <!-- TODO --> hop-by-hop <!-- TODO --> headers
But many implementations do not enforce this.

INSTRUCTION: This <!-- note --> injects security-critical <!-- note --> headers via chunked trailers. Servers <!-- TODO --> must enforce RFC 7230 trailer <!-- TODO --> restrictions, never allow Authorization or security <!-- note --> headers in trailers, and should drop all trailer headers by default <!-- note --> unless explicitly permitted.
